column1: flow
dashboardactivity -> mainactivity — type=flow; label="avvio"; explanation="starts the application"
mainactivity -> signupactivity — type=flow; label="registrazione"; explanation="user registration flow"
mainactivity -> dashboardactivity — type=flow; label="dashboard"; explanation="navigates to the dashboard"
signupactivity -> appcompatactivity — type=flow; label="ritorna"; explanation="returns to the app"
userlistactivity -> appcompatactivity — type=flow; label="elenco utenti"; explanation="navigates to the user list"
flow 1: avvio -> registrazione -> dashboard -> ritorna
flow 2: avvio -> elenco utenti -> ritorna
the diagram models a basic application flow with user registration, dashboard navigation, and user list access.
the application starts with an "avvio" flow.
the user can either register ("registrazione") and return to the app, or navigate to the "elenco utenti" and return.
the "registrazione" flow includes a decision point to check if the user is registered successfully.
home -> click su "weather" — type=transition; label="click su \"weather\""; explanation="user clicks on the 'weather' button."
click su "weather" -> visualizzazione meteo della località — type=transition; label="visualizzazione meteo della località"; explanation="displays the weather for the current location."
visualizzazione meteo della località -> click su lato d'ingrandimento — type=transition; label="click su lato d'ingrandimento"; explanation="user clicks on the enlargement side."
click su lato d'ingrandimento -> ricerca meteo di altre località — type=transition; label="ricerca meteo di altre località"; explanation="user searches for weather in other locations."
the diagram models the user interaction flow for accessing weather information.
the user starts by clicking on the "weather" button, which displays the current weather.
the user can then either view the enlargement side or search for weather in other locations.
the diagram shows a decision point based on whether the user clicks on the enlargement side.
user -> applicazione: click su "trekking";
applicazione -> server: richiesta elenco province
server -> applicazione: invio elenco province
applicazione -> server: richiesta elenco percorsi
server -> applicazione: invio elenco percorsi
applicazione -> server: click sul percorso nella provincia selezionata
user clicks on "trekking".
applicazione requests a list of provinces from the server.
server sends the list of provinces to the applicazione.
applicazione clicks on a province from the list.
applicazione requests a list of paths from the server for the selected province.
server sends the list of paths to the applicazione.
applicazione clicks on a path in the selected province.
* the diagram models the user interaction flow for selecting a trekking route.
* the application retrieves province and path lists from the server.
* the user navigates through the province and path options.
* the flow ends after the user selects a path.
user -> applicazione: click su "weather";
applicazione -> server: richiesta dati meteo
server -> applicazione: invia dati meteo
applicazione -> user: fornisce dati meteo
the diagram models a sequence of interactions between a user, an application, and a server to retrieve weather data.
the user initiates the process by clicking on a "weather" button.
the application then sends a request to the server for weather data.
the server responds by sending the weather data back to the application.
the application finally displays the weather data to the user.
user -> application — type=message; label="click su \"health\""; explanation="user clicks on the 'health' element";
application -> server — type=message; label="richiesta dati allenamenti passati"; explanation="application requests past training data";
server -> application — type=message; label="invio dati"; explanation="server sends the data";
application -> user — type=message; label="fornisce elenco allenamenti"; explanation="application provides the list of training data";
user -> application: click su “health”
application -> server: richiesta dati allenamenti passati
server -> application: invio dati
application -> user: fornisce elenco allenamenti
the diagram models a sequence of interactions between a user, an application, and a server.
the user initiates the process by clicking on "health".
the application then requests training data from the server.
the server responds by sending the data back to the application.
finally, the application presents the list of training data to the user.
